title: v6.0 sudo pipe + encoding fixes (2026-04-20)
desc: Three bugs fixed: wkappbot_elevated pipe routing, --sudo proxy Korean mojibake, and CreateProcessA argv UTF-8 recovery.
steps:
FIX 1: wkappbot_elevated pipe routing (root cause of 30s proxy timeout).
Normal Eye was opening ElevatedEyeServer on wkappbot_elevated alongside admin Eye.
Client connected to whichever accepted first -- normal Eye intercepted, then spawned
non-elevated subprocess which timed out (stdoutDone=False stderrDone=False).
Solution: normal Eye -> wkappbot_eye_ipc (tick IPC only). Admin Eye -> wkappbot_elevated.
Files: AppBotEyeGlobalMode.cs L613, ElevatedEyeProxy.cs (new EyeIpcServer class).

FIX 2: --sudo proxy Korean output mojibake.
ProcessStartInfo default StandardOutputEncoding = system ACP (CP949 on Korean Windows).
Subprocess wrote UTF-8 but admin Eye parent read bytes as CP949 -> mojibake.
Solution: psi.StandardOutputEncoding = Encoding.UTF8 in ElevatedEyeProxy.cs ExecuteCommand.

FIX 3: Korean argv from CreateProcessA.
CreateProcessA converts UTF-8 bytes through CP949 to wide chars, corrupting Korean args[].
Solution: TryRecoverUtf8Argv() in Program.cs, runs at Main() entry before any arg inspection.
Algorithm: GetCommandLineA() raw bytes -> strict UTF-8 decode -> if valid: CommandLineToArgvW re-parse.
CP949 Korean lead bytes (0xBE, 0xB3...) fail UTF-8 check immediately -> CreateProcessW callers pass through unchanged.
NativeMethods added: GetCommandLineA, CommandLineToArgvW, LocalFree (shell32+kernel32).
author: claude
